codeReview.summary: The feature adds a performant canvas graph and detail panel, but the graph model keys entities only by lowercase label. That collapses distinct people/organizations/locations sharing the same name into one node, which breaks the promised type separation and can hide real co-occurrence edges.

issues.description: Entity deduplication is keyed only by `name.toLowerCase()` and the same scheme is repeated for people, organizations, and locations. Distinct typed entities such as an organization and a location both named "Georgia" collapse into a single node, inherit whichever type was seen first, and lose the edge that should connect them. A direct check with `extractEntityGraph()` on one organization+location event returns just one node and zero edges, so the type filter and graph structure are not reliable for same-label cross-type entities.
issues.suggestedFix: Key nodes and event-local entity references by both normalized type and normalized name (for example `organization:georgia` vs `location:georgia`) while preserving the display label separately, then update filtering and related-event lookups to use the typed key.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Canvas-only UI verification needs a more explicit fallback path in the frontend-worker skill. The entity graph rendered on a single `<canvas>`, so the worker had to use coordinate-based clicks and DOM/eval inspection instead of normal locator-driven browser automation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff 2026-03-27T13-20-30-799Z__entity-relationship-graph__7085ce0e-cf06-4ba0-b78b-e3d37be5b030.json records this in `skillFeedback.suggestedChanges`, noting that canvas-based components could not be validated with standard agent-browser element interaction.

summary: Fail. Same-label entities from different types collapse into one node, so the graph does not reliably preserve people/org/location distinctions.
